marketFocus: Iran IRGC closes Strait of Hormuz: 'any traffic will be severely dealt with.' US bases attacked in Saudi, Kuwait, Qatar, Bahrain, UAE. Brent above $102/bbl. Trump extended 10-day pause but mixed signals persist — escalation risk maximum.

UK Retail Sales Feb beat (-0.4% vs -0.8% exp, YoY 2.5% vs 2.1% exp). BOE Breeden: 'not wise to act before sufficient information' — April meeting in focus. GBP Bullish — stagflation + hawkish BOE intact.

UBS upgrades ECB to two 25bp hikes (June + September). Spanish CPI prelim hot (3.3% YoY vs 2.4% exp). Core PCE at 13:30 FFT — key USD catalyst. Fed speakers (Barr, Cook, Jefferson) all hawkish on inflation.

USD.tone: DXY Rising on Haven + Rate Hike Bets — Core PCE the Key Catalyst
USD.summary: USD is Bullish today. DXY climbed alongside higher oil prices and rising yields as Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz and US bases were attacked across the Middle East. Fed speakers Barr, Cook and Jefferson all struck hawkish tones — Cook: 'inflation risk greater now as a result of the war in Iran.' Markets now price 15bps of hikes by year-end. Core PCE at 13:30 FFT is the key event — a hot print would cement the bullish case. Weekly Neutral vs Daily Bullish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: DXY rising as Hormuz closure and US base attacks drive haven demand
Fed Barr: 'longer inflation above 2%, greater risk it becomes entrenched'
Fed Cook: 'inflation risk greater now as a result of the war in Iran'
Markets price 15bps of Fed hikes by year-end on energy inflation fears
Core PCE Price Index m/m at 13:30 FFT — key catalyst for USD direction

EUR.tone: UBS ECB Hike Upgrade + Hot Spanish CPI — EUR Hawkish Narrative Intact
EUR.summary: EUR is Bullish today. UBS upgraded its ECB forecast to two 25bp hikes in June and September (vs previously unchanged). Spanish CPI prelim came in hot at 3.3% YoY (vs 2.4% exp) — the energy shock is feeding into Eurozone inflation. ECB Patsalides: 'will not hesitate to hike.' EUR/USD held above 1.1522 despite broad risk-off. The energy importer headwind is offset by the rates repricing story. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: UBS upgrades ECB to two 25bp hikes: June and September 2026
Spanish CPI prelim 3.3% YoY (vs 2.4% exp) — energy shock feeding into EZ inflation
ECB Patsalides: 'will not hesitate to hike' despite uncertainty
ECB Nagel: 'will have enough data by April to determine if they need to act'
EUR/USD holding above 1.1522 despite risk-off — rates repricing dominates

GBP.tone: Retail Sales Beat + BOE Hawkish — GBP Outperforming in Risk-Off
GBP.summary: GBP is Bullish today. UK Retail Sales Feb beat: -0.4% MoM (vs -0.8% exp), +2.5% YoY (vs 2.1% exp). BOE Breeden: 'where we are now is very different to last energy shock in 2022; second-round effects less likely given lack of price and wage bargaining power.' GBP/USD traded near 1.3300 handle. The stagflation narrative (Core CPI 3.2% beat yesterday, Services 4.3% beat) combined with the hawkish BOE stance keeps GBP supported. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: UK Retail Sales Feb: -0.4% MoM (beat vs -0.8% exp), +2.5% YoY (beat vs 2.1% exp)
BOE Breeden: 'not wise to act before sufficient information' — April meeting key
BOE Breeden: 'second-round effects less likely' vs 2022 — controlled hawkish stance
Stagflation narrative intact: Core CPI 3.2% + Services 4.3% beat yesterday
GBP/USD near 1.3300; Gilts lower by ~80 ticks but off worst levels

JPY.tone: Energy Vulnerability Overwhelms Haven Bid — USD/JPY at YTD Highs
JPY.summary: JPY is Bearish today. Despite the risk-off environment, JPY is underperforming because Japan imports 90%+ of crude from the Middle East — the Hormuz closure is a direct economic shock for Japan. USD/JPY pushed to YTD highs near 159.00. Japanese Finance Minister Katayama announced measures for stable business funding and said they will take 'decisive steps on FX' at the G7 Finance Ministers' online meeting. Former BOJ Governor Kuroda: 'no problem raising policy rate 3-4 times to around 1.5% through next year.' Weekly Neutral vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Japan imports 90%+ of crude from Middle East — Hormuz closure is direct economic shock
USD/JPY at YTD highs near 159.00 — energy vulnerability overwhelms haven bid
Japanese Finance Minister Katayama: 'decisive steps on FX' at G7 Finance meeting
Former BOJ Governor Kuroda: 'no problem raising policy rate 3-4 times to ~1.5%'
BOJ neutral rate estimated -0.9% to +0.5% — tightening path gradual

AUD.tone: Cyclone Narelle Disrupts LNG + Risk-Off + China Probes US
AUD.summary: AUD is Bearish today. Cyclone Narelle has disrupted LNG production in Western Australia — Chevron halted Gorgon and Wheatstone (8% of global LNG supply). China's MOFCOM launched a probe against the US for green trade practices, adding trade uncertainty. Risk-off environment weighs on high-beta AUD. UBS now sees the RBA hiking twice more to 4.60% but this is a medium-term positive that cannot offset the immediate risk-off and supply disruption narrative. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Cyclone Narelle: Chevron halted Gorgon + Wheatstone LNG (8% of global supply)
China MOFCOM launched probe against US green trade practices — trade uncertainty
Risk-off environment weighs on high-beta AUD despite RBA rate support
UBS: RBA to hike twice more to 4.60% — medium-term positive, not today's driver
ASX 200 under pressure; gold miners and tech leading declines

CAD.tone: Hormuz Closed = Maximum Oil Risk Premium — CAD Pure Petro-Proxy
CAD.summary: CAD is Bullish today. Iran's IRGC announced the Strait of Hormuz is closed — Brent surged above $102/bbl, WTI above $94/bbl. This is the maximum bullish scenario for CAD as a petro-proxy. The Hormuz closure means ~20% of global oil supply is at risk. Macquarie's $150/bbl scenario if Hormuz stays shut is now the market's tail risk. No major CAD events today — the currency is a pure oil play. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: Iran IRGC closes Strait of Hormuz — Brent above $102/bbl, WTI above $94/bbl
Hormuz closure = ~20% of global oil supply at risk — maximum petro-proxy support
Macquarie $150/bbl tail risk scenario now in market's pricing framework
US bases attacked in Saudi, Kuwait, Qatar, Bahrain, UAE — escalation risk premium
No major CAD events — pure oil proxy, tracks Brent directly

CHF.tone: SNB Intervention Cap Limits Haven Bid Despite Risk-Off
CHF.summary: CHF is Bearish today. Despite the risk-off environment, the SNB's explicit intervention language from earlier this week caps CHF appreciation. BOE Breeden's comment that 'gilt market functioning well amid Iran war volatility' suggests the safe-haven flows are being absorbed by USD and Gilts rather than CHF. EUR/CHF stabilizing near 0.9100 as the SNB intervention threat keeps a lid on appreciation. Fitch Ratings on Switzerland today is the key event. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: SNB explicit intervention cap limits CHF haven appreciation despite risk-off
EUR/CHF stabilizing near 0.9100 — SNB intervention threat intact
Haven flows absorbed by USD and Gilts rather than CHF in this environment
Fitch Ratings on Switzerland today — any downgrade would be CHF negative
CHF appreciated 2.5% trade-weighted since December — SNB sees this as excessive

NZD.tone: Consumer Confidence Collapses + Fuel Contingency Plan — NZD Weakest G10
NZD.summary: NZD is Bearish today. New Zealand ANZ-Roy Morgan consumer confidence fell to 91.3 in March (from 100.1 in February) — a sharp 10-point drop back into pessimistic territory. New Zealand activated Phase 1 of its four-phase fuel contingency framework due to Hormuz disruption risk. RBNZ increased foreign currency intervention capacity in February. No domestic hawkish catalyst. High-beta risk proxy in a risk-off environment. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: NZ consumer confidence collapses to 91.3 (from 100.1) — back into pessimistic territory
NZ activates Phase 1 fuel contingency plan: 49-day petrol, 46-day diesel reserves
RBNZ increased FX intervention capacity in February — policy defensive
High-beta risk proxy in maximum risk-off environment
No domestic hawkish catalyst; RBNZ next meeting not until April
